title: Accelerated Trial and Disposal of Cases Rules, 1988
title_en: Accelerated Trial and Disposal of Cases Rules, 1988

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Rules may be cited as the Accelerated Trial and Disposal of Cases Rules.

provisions.title: Preliminary hearing
provisions.content: In every case where a person charged pleads not guilty to the charge the presiding magistrate or judge shall hold a preliminary hearing on the day when the person is charged or arraigned in the presence of his advocate or, if this is not possible, then as soon as it is practicable.

provisions.title: Facts of the case
provisions.content: The person prosecuting shall, in every trial under these Rules, prepare, as clearly as possible, the facts of the case which shall be read to the accused and explained in a language he understands.

provisions.title: Right to ask questions
provisions.content: It shall be lawful for the accused or the court during the reading and explaining the facts of the case, to ask questions or seek clarification on any matter relating to the facts of the case.

provisions.title: Admission of facts of case
provisions.content: When the facts of the case are read and explained to the accused, the court shall ask him to state which of those facts he admits and the trial magistrate or judge shall record the same.

provisions.title: No witnesses at preliminary hearing
provisions.content: No witness shall be called during a preliminary hearing.

provisions.title: Notice to witnesses to appear
provisions.content: The court may give notice to any person who is likely to be called as a witness after a preliminary hearing, that he may be required to give evidence before the court on a date to be specified in the notice and such notice shall be deemed to be summonses duly issued and served upon him to appear and give evidence as required in the trial.
